pending.body: src/adapters/tmux.rs:452-453 advertises `enforces_timeout() == true`, but `TmuxAdapter::invoke()` never applies `request.timeout`; it just waits in `wait_for_session_exit()` until the service cancels it (`src/adapters/tmux.rs:518-520`, `src/adapters/tmux.rs:342-379`). Because `AgentExecutionService` adds a 30-second buffer whenever that flag is true (`src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs:204-223`), tmux runs now exceed the configured timeout by about 30 seconds before cancellation. This regresses the hard-timeout behavior for the `process`/`openrouter` selector in tmux mode (`src/composition/agent_execution_builder.rs:95-108`).

src/adapters/openrouter_backend.rs:64-74 still reports a missing `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` as `BackendUnavailable { failure_class: None }`. `AppError::failure_class()` maps that to `TransportFailure` (`src/shared/error.rs:309-313`), and `RetryPolicy` now retries transport failures 5 times (`src/contexts/workflow_composition/retry_policy.rs:45-57`). In non-tmux `openrouter` mode (`src/composition/agent_execution_builder.rs:114`), a missing API key will therefore still burn the full retry budget instead of failing immediately as a terminal prerequisite error.
